protocol.name: xdg_foreign_unstable_v2

description.summary: Protocol for exporting xdg surface handles

protocol.description:
This protocol specifies a way for making it possible to reference a surface of a different client. With such a reference, a client can, by using the interfaces provided by this protocol, manipulate the relationship between its own surfaces and the surface of some other client. For example, stack some of its own surface above the other clients surface.
In order for a client A to get a reference of a surface of client B, client B must first export its surface using xdg_exporter. Upon doing this, client B will receive a handle (a unique string) that it may share with client A in some way (for example D-Bus). After client A has received the handle from client B, it may use xdg_importer to create a reference to the surface client B just exported. See the corresponding requests for details.
A possible use case for this is out-of-process dialogs. For example when a sandboxed client without file system access needs the middle-ware to show a file browser dialog, it can export its surface, passing the exported surface handle to the middle-ware, which then can use it to create a child surface for the sandboxed client, positioning the dialog box next to the sandboxed clients surface.
Warning! The protocol described in this file is experimental and backward incompatible changes may be made. Backward compatible changes may be added together with the corresponding interface version bump. Backward incompatible changes are done by bumping the version number in the protocol and interface names and resetting the interface version. Once the protocol is to be declared stable, the 'z' prefix and the version number in the protocol and interface names are removed and the interface version number is reset.

interface.name: zxdg_exporter_v2
interface.description: A global interface used for exporting surfaces that can later be imported using xdg_importer.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Notify the compositor that the xdg_exporter object will no longer be used.

request.name: export_toplevel
request.description:
The export_toplevel request exports the passed surface so that it can later be imported via xdg_importer. When called, a new xdg_exported object will be created and xdg_exported.handle will be sent immediately. See the corresponding interface and event for details.
A surface may be exported multiple times, and each exported handle may be used to create an xdg_imported multiple times. Only xdg_surface toplevel surface roles are accepted for export and the surface must not have a further role assigned.

interface.name: zxdg_importer_v2
interface.description: A global interface used for importing surfaces exported by xdg_exporter. With this interface, a client can create a reference to a surface of another client.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Notify the compositor that the xdg_importer object will no longer be used.

request.name: import_toplevel
request.description: The import_toplevel request imports a surface from any client given a handle retrieved by exporting said surface using xdg_exporter.export_toplevel. When called, a new xdg_imported object will be created. This new object represents the imported surface, and the importing client should use xdg_imported.set_parent_of to establish a surface to surface relationship between its own surfaces and the imported surface. The handle is opaque and private to the exporting client.

interface.name: zxdg_exported_v2
interface.description: A xdg_exported object represents an exported reference to a surface. The exported surface may be referenced as long as the xdg_exported object not destroyed. Destroying the xdg_exported invalidates any relationship the importer may have set up using the xdg_imported created given the same handle.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Revoke the previously exported surface. This invalidates any relationship the importer may have set up using the xdg_imported created given the same handle.

event.name: handle
event.description: The handle event contains the unique handle of this exported surface reference. It may be shared with any client, which then can use it to import the surface by calling xdg_importer.import_toplevel. A handle may be used to import the surface multiple times.

interface.name: zxdg_imported_v2
interface.description: A xdg_imported object represents an imported reference to surface exported by some client. A client can use this interface to manipulate relationships between its own surfaces and the imported surface.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Notify the compositor that it will no longer use the xdg_imported object. Any relationship that may have been set up will at this point be invalidated.

request.name: set_parent_of

description.summary: set the parent of some surface

request.description:
Set the imported surface as the parent of some surface of the client. The passed surface must be an xdg_surface child of the passed surface. Calling this function sets up a surface to surface binding which will cause the compositor to identify the parent surface's content as the parent content of the passed surface.
The compositor may change the final parent relationship to be different than the imported surface, for example due to constraints or policy.
For the purpose of this protocol, a toplevel is any surface that has the "toplevel" xdg_surface role.
The parent surface may be null in order to cancel a previously set parent surface relationship.
Setting the parent surface to null is semantically equivalent to unsetting the parent, and the compositor MAY choose to ignore requests to unset the parent if the compositor has already established a relationship between the surface and the imported surface.

event.name: destroyed

description.summary: the imported surface handle has been destroyed

event.description: The imported surface handle has been destroyed and any relationship set up has been invalidated. This may happen for various reasons, for example if the exported surface or the exported surface's client vanished.
